1.description: Timing The figure illustrates the sequence of events.

2.description: Marginal costs The figure illustrates the marginal costs and benefits of investing in risky growth opportunities for index and nonindex firms, as a function of real investment Оѕn. In each case, the black dot represents the optimal (equilibrium) real investment. The graph is based on the following parameter values: r = 1, ПЃ = 2, ПѓОјПѓПЂПѓx=0.32,вЂ‰Ојz=1.3,вЂ‰Пѓz=0.352,вЂ‰ПЂ=0.001вЃ , c = 0.5, О“P=0.2вЃ , N = 50, and M = 5.

3.description: Real investment The figure illustrates how the number of stocks in the index affects firmsвЂ™ marginal costs and real investments. Panels A and B, respectively, depict the difference in marginal costs of investing in growth opportunities and the difference in real investment between index and nonindex firms, as a function of the number of index firms M. The graphs are based on the following parameter values: r = 1, ПЃ = 2, ПѓОјПѓПЂПѓx=0.32,вЂ‰Ојz=1.3,вЂ‰Пѓz=0.352,вЂ‰ПЂ=0.001вЃ , c = 0.5, О“P=0.2вЃ , and N = 50.

4.description: Information choices The figure illustrates how the marginal benefits and costs of private information vary across index and nonindex firms. Specifically, it depicts an active investorвЂ™s marginal benefits and costs of acquiring private information, as a function of the average private signal precision K^nвЃ . The black dots represent the equilibrium information choices. The graphs are based on the following parameter values: r = 1, ПЃ = 2, ПѓОјПѓПЂПѓx=0.32,вЂ‰Ојz=1.3,вЂ‰Пѓz=0.352,вЂ‰ПЂ=0.001вЃ , c = 0.5, О“P=0.2вЃ , N = 50, and M = 5.

5.description: Informational efficiency The figure illustrates how the number of stocks in the index affects informational efficiency. Panels A and B, respectively, plot the difference in private signal precision and in price informativeness between index and nonindex firms, as a function of the number of stocks in the index M. The graphs are based on the following parameter values: r = 1, ПЃ = 2, ПѓОјПѓПЂПѓx=0.32,вЂ‰Ојz=1.3,вЂ‰Пѓz=0.352,вЂ‰ПЂ=0.001вЃ , c = 0.5, О“P=0.2вЃ , and N = 50.

6.description: Stock return moments The figure illustrates how the number of stocks in the index affects stock return moments. It plots the difference in expected excess returns (panel A), in stock return volatilities (panel B), and in stock return covariances (panel C) between index and nonindex firms, as functions of the number of index stocks M. Stock return covariances are measured within each firm group, that is, among index firms and, respectively, among nonindex firms. The graphs are based on the following parameter values: r = 1, ПЃ = 2, ПѓОјПѓПЂПѓx=0.32,вЂ‰Ојz=1.3,вЂ‰Пѓz=0.352,вЂ‰ПЂ=0.001вЃ , c = 0.5, О“P=0.2вЃ , and N = 50.

7.description: Marginal costs versus index weight The figure illustrates the marginal costs and benefits of investing in risky growth opportunities as a function of real investment Оѕn for different weights of a stock in the index. In each case, a black dot represents the optimal real investment. The graph is based on the following parameter values: r = 1, ПЃ = 2, ПѓОјПѓПЂПѓx=0.32,вЂ‰Ојz=1.3,вЂ‰Пѓz=0.352,вЂ‰ПЂ=0.001вЃ , c = 0.5, О“P=0.2вЃ , N = 50, M = 5, and w=[0.5,0.75,1.0,1.25,1.5,0,вЂ¦,0]вЂІвЃ .

8.description: Variations in index weights The figure illustrates how the weight of a stock in the index affects real investment, informational efficiency, stock prices, and stock return moments. It depicts real investment Оѕn (panel A), active investorsвЂ™ average private signal precision K^n (panel B), price informativeness PIn (panel C), the expected firm value Sn (panel D), the expected excess return ERn (panel E), and the stock return volatility ОЈn,nret (panel F), as functions of the weight of a stock in the index wn. The graphs are based on the following parameter values: r = 1, ПЃ = 2, ПѓОјПѓПЂПѓx=0.32,вЂ‰Ојz=1.3,вЂ‰Пѓz=0.352,вЂ‰ПЂ=0.001вЃ , c = 0.5, О“P=0.2вЃ , N = 50, M = 5, and w=[0.5,0.75,1.0,1.25,1.5,0,вЂ¦,0]вЂІвЃ .

9.description: Variations in the share of passive investors The figure illustrates the impact of variations in the share of passive investors. Depicted are the marginal costs of investing in growth opportunities (panel A), real investment (panel B), price informativeness (panel C), and the expected stock price (panel D) of index and nonindex firms, as a function of the share of passive investors О“PвЃ . The graphs are based on the following parameter values: r = 1, ПЃ = 2, ПѓОјПѓПЂПѓx=0.32,вЂ‰Ојz=1.3,вЂ‰Пѓz=0.352,вЂ‰ПЂ=0.001вЃ , c = 0.5, N = 50, and M = 5. In panel A, real investment, Оѕn, is set equal to 0.25.

10.description: Investment and information crashes The figure illustrates investment and information crashes. Depicted are real investment (panel A) and price informativeness (panel B) of index and nonindex firms, as a function of the share of passive investors О“PвЃ . Panel C plots the marginal costs and benefits of investing in risky growth opportunities of a nonindex firm, as a function of real investment Оѕn for two different levels of the share of passive investors. The graphs are based on the following parameter values: r = 1, ПЃ = 2, ПѓОјПѓПЂПѓx=0.32,вЂ‰Ојz=1.3,вЂ‰Пѓz=0.352,вЂ‰ПЂ=0.001вЃ , c = 0.4, N = 50, and M = 5.

A1.description: Marginal costs with systematic risk The figure illustrates the marginal costs and benefits of investing in risky growth opportunities of index and nonindex firms, as a function of real investment Оѕn. It is drawn for the case with systematic risk. In each case, the black dot represents the optimal real investment. The graph is based on the following parameter values: r = 1, ПЃ = 2, ПѓОјПѓПЂПѓx=0.32,вЂ‰Ојz=1.3,вЂ‰Пѓz=0.352,вЂ‰ПЂ=0.001вЃ , c = 0.5, О“P=0.2вЃ , N = 50, M = 5, and ПѓПѓy=0.252вЃ .

A2.description: Variations in systematic risk The figure illustrates how systematic risk affects real investment, private-signal precision, and informational efficiency. Depicted are the difference in real investment Оѕn (panel A), in active investorsвЂ™ average private signal precision K^n (panel B), and in price informativeness PIn (panel C) between index and nonindex firms, as a function of the aggregate noise variance Пѓy. Panel D plots the difference in real investment Оѕn between index and nonindex firms, as a function of the number of index firms M. The graphs are based on the following parameter values: r = 1, ПЃ = 2, ПѓОјПѓПЂПѓx=0.32,вЂ‰Ојz=1.3,вЂ‰Пѓz=0.352,вЂ‰ПЂ=0.001вЃ , c = 0.5, О“P=0.2вЃ , N = 50, and M = 5 (for panels A to C).
